items.change_action: must equal mapped constant for Heartbeat demultiplexing
items.comment: Heartbeat appears in the RFC demux table, but BoringSSL has no Heartbeat feature or handler. Unsupported Heartbeat records are rejected/not dispatched, while processing Heartbeat records is unavailable.
items.comparison_summary: Requirement: when OCT == 24, Outer Content Type must must equal mapped constant for Heartbeat demultiplexing. Input evidence: OCT == 24   -+--> Heartbeat (DTLS <1.3). Code evidence: boringssl-main/ssl/dtls_record.cc:267; boringssl-main/ssl/d1_both.cc:419; boringssl-main/ssl/d1_pkt.cc:223. Heartbeat appears in the RFC demux table, but BoringSSL has no Heartbeat feature or handler. Unsupported Heartbeat records are rejected/not dispatched, while processing Heartbeat records is unavailable. Conclusion: partially satisfied.
items.standard_check: RFC 9147 Figure 5 maps OCT 24 and decrypted content type 24 to Heartbeat, but Heartbeat is an optional TLS/DTLS feature rather than mandatory DTLS 1.3 core behavior.
items.code_check: Static re-read found no SSL3_RT_HEARTBEAT or heartbeat handler. DTLS dispatch handles handshake, ACK, application data, alert, and CCS-specific paths.
items.test_check: focused_static_id152_153_185_187.py PASS and linked C harness PASS: heartbeat symbols and dispatch path are absent.
items.decision_reason: Unsupported Heartbeat is safely rejected/not dispatched, but the implementation cannot process Heartbeat records if that feature is required.

items.change_action: must equal mapped constant for encrypted_record with CID demultiplexing
items.comment: BoringSSL has no DTLS 1.2 tls12_cid demux path, rejects DTLS 1.3 records with the C bit set, and always sends DTLS 1.3 encrypted records with C=0.
items.comparison_summary: Requirement: when OCT == 25, Outer Content Type must must equal mapped constant for encrypted_record with CID demultiplexing. Input evidence: OCT == 25   -+--> DTLSCiphertext with CID (DTLS 1.2). Code evidence: boringssl-main/ssl/dtls_record.cc:170; boringssl-main/ssl/dtls_record.cc:431; boringssl-main/ssl/dtls_record.cc:533. BoringSSL has no DTLS 1.2 tls12_cid demux path, rejects DTLS 1.3 records with the C bit set, and always sends DTLS 1.3 encrypted records with C=0. Conclusion: not satisfied.
items.standard_check: RFC 9147 Section 4.1 includes OCT 25 for DTLSCiphertext with CID in DTLS 1.2, and DTLS 1.3 has an explicit CID bit in encrypted record headers.
items.code_check: parse_dtls13_record rejects the C bit, the sender fixes C=0, and no tls12_cid demux symbols are present.
items.test_check: focused_static_id152_153_185_187.py PASS and linked C harness PASS: CID symbols are absent, C-bit rejection exists, and the send header is fixed to C=0.

items.change_condition: in some cases it may be necessary to send an ACK which does not contain any record numbers
items.evidence_from_input: it may be necessary to send an ACK which does not contain any record numbers.
items.comment: ACK encoding can represent a length-prefixed record_numbers vector, but dtls1_schedule_ack only sends ACKs when records_to_ack is non-empty and send_ack assumes space for at least one RecordNumber.
items.comparison_summary: Requirement: when in some cases it may be necessary to send an ACK which does not contain any record numbers, record_numbers must set to empty / absent. Input evidence: it may be necessary to send an ACK which does not contain any record numbers.. Code evidence: boringssl-main/ssl/d1_both.cc:956; boringssl-main/ssl/d1_both.cc:969; boringssl-main/ssl/d1_both.cc:990. ACK encoding can represent a length-prefixed record_numbers vector, but dtls1_schedule_ack only sends ACKs when records_to_ack is non-empty and send_ack assumes space for at least one RecordNumber. Conclusion: partially satisfied.
items.standard_check: RFC 9147 allows an ACK that contains no record numbers in special cases.
items.code_check: The ACK vector format is length-prefixed, but scheduling is gated by records_to_ack being non-empty and send_ack assumes at least one RecordNumber can fit.
items.test_check: focused_static_id157_empty_ack.py PASS and linked C harness PASS: empty-ACK scheduling path is absent.
items.decision_reason: Normal ACK generation works, but the special empty ACK shortcut is not implemented.

items.evidence_from_input: If it is set to "cid_spare", then either an existing or new CID MAY be used.
items.comment: NewConnectionId usage=cid_spare cannot be implemented because BoringSSL has no DTLS CID update state machine and no CID-capable record support. Root cause same as ID153.
items.comparison_summary: Requirement: when If it is set to "cid_spare", usage must must equal / selected behavior. Input evidence: If it is set to "cid_spare", then either an existing or new CID MAY be used.. Code evidence: boringssl-main/ssl/dtls_record.cc:170; boringssl-main/ssl/dtls_record.cc:431; boringssl-main/ssl/dtls_record.cc:533. NewConnectionId usage=cid_spare cannot be implemented because BoringSSL has no DTLS CID update state machine and no CID-capable record support. Root cause same as ID153. Conclusion: not satisfied.
items.standard_check: RFC 9147 Section 9 defines Connection ID update behavior using NewConnectionId and RequestConnectionId.
items.code_check: No RequestConnectionId/NewConnectionId/ConnectionIdUsage/cid_spare/cid_immediate symbols or state machine are present, and record CID support is absent.
items.test_check: focused_static_id152_153_185_187.py PASS and linked C harness PASS: CID update symbols and CID-capable record support are absent.
items.decision_reason: NewConnectionId usage=cid_spare cannot be implemented because BoringSSL has no DTLS CID update state machine and no CID-capable record support. Root cause same as ID153.

items.evidence_from_input: If usage is set to "cid_immediate", then one of the new CIDs MUST be used immediately for all future records.
items.comment: cid_immediate switching cannot be implemented because the sender always uses C=0 and there is no state transition to select a new CID for future records. Root cause same as ID153.

items.related_state_or_step: Sending NewConnectionId in response to RequestConnectionId
items.evidence_from_input: Endpoints SHOULD respond to RequestConnectionId by sending a NewConnectionId with usage "cid_spare" containing num_cids CIDs as soon as possible.
items.comment: RequestConnectionId cannot trigger a cid_spare NewConnectionId response because RequestConnectionId/NewConnectionId handling and CID-capable records are absent. Root cause same as ID153.
